heading: 5.2 Licensing — What You Get with Copilot
content: Every M365 Copilot licence automatically includes the Viva Insights service plan at no additional cost. This covers all analytics capabilities. A separate Viva Insights purchase is only needed to analyse employees who do not have Copilot licences.

heading: 6. Post-Upload Verification Checklist
content: After every upload, complete this checklist to confirm data quality before relying on dashboards.

heading: Step 1: Check Import Status
content: Status Meaning Action Success All rows processed. No errors. Proceed to Step 2. Partial Success Most rows processed. Some rows had warnings (e.g., unmatched manager emails). Download import log (Step 2). Fix and re-upload problem rows. Failed File rejected. No data was imported. Check error detai...

heading: Step 2: Download Import Log
content: Look for rows with unmatched Microsoft_ManagerEmail values. These indicate managers whose email addresses don't exist in the uploaded data or in Entra ID.

heading: Step 3: Download Manager Log
content: This log shows employees whose ManagerEmail doesn't resolve to an active user in Entra ID. These employees will appear as "orphaned" in the manager hierarchy.

heading: Step 4: Check Attribute Coverage
content: Navigate to the Data Quality tab. Every attribute should show >95% coverage (percentage of rows with non-blank values). Low coverage means dashboard filters will show incomplete results.

heading: Step 5: Check Manager Tree Count
content: The number of root nodes in the manager hierarchy should be small (typically 1–5, representing CEO/top executives). If you see hundreds or thousands, many ManagerEmail values are missing or incorrect.

heading: Step 6: Spot-Check Dashboard
content: Open the Copilot Dashboard and verify the Organisation filter shows your uploaded values (not just Entra defaults) Confirm new employees appear in the population count Check that Job Function, Location, and other optional filters are populated (if uploaded)

heading: Step 7: Approve Connector Data (If Applicable)
content: If using an HRIS connector (SAP SuccessFactors or Workday) with "Manually approve" enabled: you have a 48-hour review window to inspect the data before it goes live. If not approved within 48 hours, the data is automatically approved.

heading: 1. Privacy Thresholds
content: Privacy thresholds ensure that no individual user's behaviour is identifiable in aggregated results. Any group below the configured minimum has its data suppressed entirely. Setting Default Configurable Range Minimum group size 10 users 5–unlimited (lowest allowed = 5)

heading: How Thresholds Work
content: Data suppression: Results for groups below the threshold are suppressed — they show as blank or "insufficient data" rather than displaying potentially identifiable information. No individual visibility: No individual user behaviour is ever visible in the Copilot Dashboard, Power BI reports, or qu...

heading: 2. Exclusion Lists
content: Exclusion lists remove specific users from all Copilot Analytics and Viva Insights reporting. Excluded users' activity data is not collected, processed, or displayed anywhere.

heading: What Gets Excluded
content: Copilot Dashboard — excluded users do not appear in any dashboard metric Viva Insights behavioural reports (collaboration hours, meeting patterns, etc.) M365 Copilot Chat (web) usage data Advanced Analytics query results (Power BI person queries, meeting queries, etc.)

heading: What It Does
content: Admins can define DLP policies that detect and restrict prompts containing sensitive information types (SITs) . When a user submits a Copilot prompt that matches a DLP policy, Copilot will not respond to the prompt and will not use it for web grounding.

heading: 3. Step-by-Step: Create a Report
content: This walkthrough uses the M365 Copilot Impact template as the example, but the process is similar for all templates.

heading: Step 1 — Sign In to the Analyst Experience
content: Navigate to analysis.insights.viva.office.com and sign in with your organisational account that has the Insights Analyst role.

heading: Step 2 — Start a New Analysis
content: Select Create analysis → Templates → Copilot → choose the desired template (e.g., M365 Copilot Impact ) → Set up analysis .

heading: Step 3 — Configure Query Settings
content: Set the following: Query name — use a descriptive name (e.g., "Copilot Impact Q1 2026 — All Agencies") Time period — select the analysis window (up to 13 months of historical data) Auto-refresh — enable if you want the query to re-run automatically on a schedule Description — optional but recomme...

heading: Step 4 — Review Predefined Metrics
content: The template pre-selects the required metrics (shown as grey tags — these cannot be removed). You can optionally add extra metrics to include in the query output.

heading: Step 5 — Set Employee Filters
content: Apply filters to scope the query population: "Is Active" — always recommended to exclude departed employees Org attributes — filter by department, agency, location, or any uploaded org data attribute as needed

heading: Step 6 — Add Organisational Attributes
content: Select up to 20 organisational attributes to include in the query output. These attributes become available as group-by and filter dimensions in Power BI. Include any attribute you may want to slice by later — you cannot add them after the query runs.

heading: Step 7 — Run the Query
content: Select Run . Depending on population size and time window, the query may take minutes to hours to complete. You will receive a notification when results are ready. Data Quality Warnings During query setup, the system may flag data quality issues: Yellow warning — low quality or partial coverage f...

heading: 4. Connect Power BI
content: Once your query completes, connect it to the Power BI template:

heading: Step 1 — Download the Template
content: From the query results page in Viva Insights, download the .pbit template file.

heading: Step 2 — Open in Power BI Desktop
content: Open the downloaded .pbit file in Power BI Desktop. When prompted, paste: Partition ID — found in the Viva Insights analyst experience Query ID — found on the query results page

heading: Step 3 — Set Minimum Group Size
content: Set the minimum group size for privacy thresholds (e.g., 10). Groups smaller than this threshold will be suppressed in the report. Click Load .

heading: Step 4 — Sign In
content: When prompted for credentials, select Organisational account and sign in with the same account used to run the query.

heading: Step 5 — Configure Report Settings
content: After data loads, configure the report settings on the Settings page: Time period — define the reporting window within the query's date range Group-by attribute — select the primary organisational attribute for segmentation (e.g., Department, Agency) Active user definition — set the threshold for...

content: You can share query results with business leaders directly from Viva Insights using the Publish feature.

heading: Step 1 — Select Publish
content: In the Viva Insights analyst experience, navigate to your completed query and select Publish .

heading: Step 2 — Set Report Metadata
content: Enter: Title — the report title that recipients will see Publisher name — your name or team name Description — brief summary of what the report covers

heading: Step 3 — Choose Scope
content: Select one of: Unfiltered original — publishes the full dataset. All recipients see the same data. Filter by manager hierarchy — each recipient sees only data for their reporting line.

heading: Step 4 — Set Expiry Date
content: Set when the published report expires. Maximum is 365 days from publication.

heading: Step 5 — Publish
content: Confirm and publish. Recipients receive an email notification with a link to view the report. No Auto-Notification Before Expiry Published reports expire silently — there is no automatic notification sent before a report expires. heading: 4. Copilot Credits — Understanding Consumption
content: Copilot Credits are the consumption currency for Copilot Studio agent interactions and certain M365 Copilot Chat scenarios. Understanding what costs credits — and what doesn't — is essential for budget management.

heading: What Costs Credits

heading: 4. Copilot Credits — Understanding Consumption
content: Copilot Credits are the consumption currency for Copilot Studio agent interactions and certain M365 Copilot Chat scenarios. Understanding what costs credits — and what doesn't — is essential for budget management.

heading: What Costs Credits
content: Agent responses using Copilot Studio capabilities beyond zero-rated usage Tenant data grounding (SharePoint, Graph) in M365 Copilot Chat (web-based) Custom agent actions, plugins, and connector invocations Generative answers using non-M365 knowledge sources

heading: Zero-Rated (No Credits Required)

heading: Credit Mechanics
content: Credits reset monthly — there is no rollover of unused credits to the next month. Overage behaviour: when credits are exhausted, model runs are blocked until more credits are purchased or allocated. content: For organisations with multiple agencies, departments, or cost centres, a chargeback model distributes Copilot costs to the consuming business units.

heading: Option B: Separate Billing Policies
content: Create separate billing policies in PPAC — one per agency. Link each billing policy to a different Azure subscription or cost centre. Assign environments to the appropriate billing policy based on the owning agency. Each agency's Azure subscription is billed directly for their consumption.
